# Claim-Evidence Framework

## Claim levels

| Level | Meaning | Draft Language |
|---|---|---|
| Observed result | Directly produced by data or experiment | “We observed...” |
| Supported inference | Reasonable interpretation from evidence | “These results suggest...” |
| Comparative claim | Supported by baseline/control comparison | “Compared with...” |
| Mechanistic claim | Requires strong causal or process evidence | Use only with appropriate design |
| Speculation | Plausible but not tested | “One possible explanation is...” |

## Evidence bank schema

| Evidence ID | Source/File | Result Type | What It Shows | Related Claim | Strength | Limitations | Figure/Table Candidate | Verification Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## Claim register schema

| Claim ID | Draft Claim | Claim Level | Evidence IDs | Citation IDs | Required Qualification | Forbidden Overclaim | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|

## Sentence-level discipline

Every nontrivial statement in the draft must be one of:

- supported by user evidence;
- supported by a verified citation;
- explicitly marked as interpretation;
- explicitly marked as limitation or speculation;
- removed.
